analyses.name: Liquidia Corporation
analyses.verdict: The cleanest technical setup in the whole scan, backed by an exploding revenue base as its lead drug ramps.

financials.summary: Liquidia is a specialty pharma company that only recently flipped from a clinical-stage story to a commercial one following FDA approval of Yutrepia (inhaled treprostinil) for pulmonary arterial hypertension and PH-ILD. FY2025 was the first real commercial ramp year, so revenue growth off a tiny base looks explosive, and the company is still working toward sustained profitability as it scales the launch.
highlights: Revenue growth of roughly 40x+ YoY off a small prior-year base as Yutrepia's launch ramped
Yutrepia priced competitively against United Therapeutics' Tyvaso to take share in PAH/PH-ILD
Forward P/E near 14x despite the growth rate, reflecting the market still discounting launch execution risk

accomplishments: FDA final approval and commercial launch of Yutrepia after a long patent-litigation delay with United Therapeutics
Rapid prescriber and payer coverage build-out in the months following launch
Stock resolved a multi-year VCP base and pushed to fresh 52-week highs on the launch news flow

journey_52w.summary: LQDA spent most of the past year basing and building contractions while the Yutrepia launch ramped quietly, then broke out hard as prescription trends and payer coverage data confirmed the drug was taking real share — the stock is now within 1% of its 52-week high on a 5-contraction VCP.

milestones.label: Early 2026
milestones.note: Yutrepia prescription and payer-coverage data began confirming a real commercial ramp

milestones.label: Recent weeks
milestones.note: Stock broke out of a tight, low-volatility base to fresh 52-week highs

risks: Ongoing patent litigation overhang with United Therapeutics
Volume-on-breakout criterion failed in the scan — the move hasn't yet been confirmed by heavy volume
Thin float / small-cap volatility
catalysts: Continued Yutrepia prescription and payer-coverage growth data
Potential label expansion or new indication data
watch: Confirm the breakout holds above $79 on rising volume
critical.valuation: ~14x forward earnings is cheap for the growth rate, but the multiple reflects real execution and litigation risk in a single-product commercial ramp.

analyses.name: Illumina, Inc.
analyses.verdict: The genomics-sequencing leader is breaking to fresh highs with the tape and TradingView both in full agreement.

financials.summary: Illumina's core sequencing-instrument and consumables business has stabilized after a multi-year post-pandemic digestion period, with the company now growing again as new-generation sequencers (NovaSeq X platform) drive a consumables-pull-through cycle, alongside continued cost discipline post the GRAIL divestiture.
highlights: Mid-single-digit revenue growth (~5%) as sequencing volumes normalize
NovaSeq X platform driving a consumables replacement cycle
Operating margins improving as the GRAIL-related cost drag fully rolls off

accomplishments: Full separation from GRAIL completed, simplifying the balance sheet and cost structure
Continued NovaSeq X installed-base growth
Stock confirming a fresh RS new high with a clean VCP base

journey_52w.summary: ILMN spent the year working through the post-GRAIL cleanup and a sequencing-market digestion period, then built a base and broke to new highs as consumables pull-through data confirmed the NovaSeq X cycle is durable.

milestones.label: Throughout 2025
milestones.note: GRAIL divestiture cleanup and cost discipline improved margins

milestones.label: Recent months
milestones.note: NovaSeq X consumables pull-through confirmed, RS turned to new highs

risks: RSI at 68 — not cheap short-term
Genomics/biotech funding cycles (academic and biopharma R&D budgets) drive instrument demand and can swing
catalysts: New instrument or software platform announcements
critical.valuation: ~32x forward earnings — a premium multiple that assumes the growth reacceleration is durable, not a one-quarter blip.

analyses.name: Eli Lilly and Company
analyses.verdict: The GLP-1 franchise keeps compounding, and TradingView's strongest possible rating backs the tape at new highs.

financials.summary: Lilly's FY2025 was defined by continued explosive growth in its incretin franchise (Mounjaro/Zepbound), with manufacturing capacity finally catching up to demand, driving both top-line acceleration and margin expansion as the mix shifts toward the higher-margin GLP-1 products.
highlights: Revenue growth of roughly 55% YoY, driven overwhelmingly by Mounjaro and Zepbound
Continued capacity expansion easing prior supply constraints
EPS growth outpacing revenue growth as GLP-1 mix improves gross margin

accomplishments: Continued global capacity build-out for incretin manufacturing
Ongoing pipeline progress in oral GLP-1 candidates and next-generation obesity/diabetes therapies
Stock confirming fresh 52-week highs with RS leadership intact

journey_52w.summary: LLY has been one of the market's steadiest large-cap compounders over the past year, grinding to new highs as GLP-1 demand and capacity both scaled, with periodic pullbacks on competitive or pricing headlines that have so far been bought.

milestones.label: Throughout 2025
milestones.note: Sequential capacity expansion reduced Zepbound/Mounjaro supply constraints

milestones.label: Ongoing
milestones.note: Oral GLP-1 pipeline progress continues to add optionality

risks: Priced for perfection at ~27x forward earnings
Any GLP-1 competitive (Novo Nordisk) or Medicare/government drug-pricing headline is a real, market-moving risk
catalysts: New indication approvals for the incretin franchise
watch: Competitive launch cadence from Novo Nordisk
critical.valuation: Premium multiple reflecting best-in-class growth; leaves little room for a growth disappointment.

analyses.name: The Bank of New York Mellon Corporation
analyses.verdict: The single highest-scoring name in this scan — a custody-bank breakout with no fundamental red flags.

financials.summary: BNY Mellon's custody, asset-servicing and markets businesses benefited from higher asset values (record equity markets lifting fee-based AUC/AUM revenue) and steady net interest income, with continued expense discipline driving positive operating leverage.
highlights: Revenue growth of roughly 13% YoY, aided by higher markets levels lifting fee income
Continued positive operating leverage from expense discipline
Assets under custody/administration near record levels

accomplishments: Continued build-out of digital-asset custody and platform initiatives
Record or near-record assets under custody as markets rallied
Technical breakout to fresh 52-week highs with RS confirmed

journey_52w.summary: BK has quietly been one of the strongest large-cap financial performers over the past year, tracking the broader market's grind higher as custody fee income scaled with asset values, culminating in a clean breakout to new highs.

milestones.label: Throughout 2025
milestones.note: Rising equity markets lifted fee-based custody/AUC revenue

milestones.label: Recent weeks
milestones.note: Stock broke out to fresh 52-week highs on a confirmed VCP

risks: No TradingView cross-check available this run
Custody/fee revenue is levered to market levels — a broad market drawdown would hit fee income directly
catalysts: Continued AUC/AUM growth as markets rise
critical.valuation: ~14x forward earnings — reasonable for a franchise with this quality and growth.

analyses.name: Fifth Third Bancorp
analyses.verdict: A regional-bank breakout confirmed by RS at new highs and a cheap valuation.

financials.summary: Fifth Third grew loans and fee income while net interest margin stabilized/expanded as the rate environment settled, with credit quality remaining benign — a solid regional-bank print that supported the technical breakout.
highlights: Revenue growth of roughly 33% YoY (aided by fee-income lines and NIM expansion)
Net interest margin stabilizing as the rate cycle matured
Credit quality metrics remained benign, no notable deterioration

accomplishments: Continued digital-banking and commercial-payments platform investment
Stock confirming RS new highs alongside the broader regional-bank leadership group

journey_52w.summary: FITB tracked the regional-bank group's recovery over the past year as rate-cut expectations stabilized net interest margins, breaking out to new highs alongside peers like NTRS and JPM.

milestones.label: Throughout 2025
milestones.note: NIM stabilization as the rate-cut cycle matured

milestones.label: Recent weeks
milestones.note: Breakout to new highs confirmed by RS

risks: RSI 70 — stretched near-term
Regional banks remain rate-sensitive and credit-cycle sensitive
catalysts: Continued NIM expansion if rates stabilize/decline
critical.valuation: ~11.6x forward earnings — cheap for the growth and quality on display.

analyses.name: Snap-On Incorporated
analyses.verdict: As clean a technical consensus as the scan produced — TradingView shows zero sell votes.

financials.summary: Snap-on's tool business to professional technicians held up well, with steady mid-single-digit revenue growth and continued margin resilience from its franchise-distribution model, which tends to be more insulated from broad consumer-discretionary swings than typical retail.
highlights: Revenue growth of roughly 5% YoY, steady rather than explosive
Margins held up well via the franchise-van distribution model
Continued buyback activity supporting EPS

accomplishments: Continued expansion of the franchise/van-distribution network
Stock confirming fresh 52-week highs with RS at new highs

journey_52w.summary: SNA has been a steady, low-drama compounder over the past year, grinding higher with the industrials group and confirming new highs recently as RS turned decisively positive.

milestones.label: Recent weeks
milestones.note: Breakout to fresh 52-week highs with RS confirmed

risks: RSI 72 — overbought, real chase risk if it gaps further
~19x forward earnings for mid-single-digit growth leaves less margin for error
catalysts: Any pickup in professional-technician tool spending
watch: Wait for a pullback toward the 21-day rather than chasing at current levels
critical.valuation: Fair-to-full for the growth rate; the appeal here is quality/consistency, not a valuation discount.

analyses.name: Duke Energy Corporation
analyses.verdict: Utilities are quietly leading this market, and Duke has TradingView's strongest possible rating.

financials.summary: Duke Energy delivered steady regulated-utility growth, with rate-base expansion (driven in part by rising electricity demand from data centers/AI compute buildout in its service territories) supporting consistent double-digit revenue growth and reaffirmed long-term EPS growth guidance.
highlights: Revenue growth of roughly 11% YoY, aided by rate-base growth and rising demand
Reaffirmed long-term EPS growth guidance range
Data-center/AI-driven load growth emerging as an incremental demand tailwind in its territories

accomplishments: Continued grid-modernization and rate-base investment execution
Incremental large-load (data center) interconnection agreements
Stock confirming TradingView's top rating with 17 buy vs. 0 sell votes

journey_52w.summary: DUK has been a steady, low-volatility outperformer as the market rotated into defensive utilities amid falling volatility, with the AI/data-center power-demand narrative adding a genuine incremental growth angle to what was historically a pure bond-proxy story.

milestones.label: Throughout 2025
milestones.note: Data-center/large-load demand growth began showing up in service-territory forecasts

milestones.label: Recent weeks
milestones.note: Stock near fresh highs with TradingView's top rating

risks: RSI 67 — some near-term extension
Rate-sensitive if long yields rise meaningfully
critical.valuation: ~18x forward earnings — a premium to historical utility multiples, reflecting the AI-power-demand re-rating across the sector.

analyses.name: Terreno Realty Corporation
analyses.verdict: The industrial-logistics REIT leading the real-estate sector, with TradingView's top rating.

financials.summary: Terreno's infill-industrial portfolio (concentrated in coastal gateway markets) continued to see strong leasing spreads and occupancy as e-commerce and logistics tenants competed for scarce, well-located warehouse space.
highlights: Revenue growth of roughly 13% YoY
Strong releasing spreads on infill-industrial properties
High occupancy maintained across its coastal-market portfolio

accomplishments: Continued disciplined acquisition of infill-industrial properties in supply-constrained coastal markets
Stock breaking to new highs with TradingView's 17 buy / 0 sell rating

journey_52w.summary: TRNO tracked the broader industrial-REIT recovery over the past year as logistics demand normalized post-pandemic overbuild fears, confirming a breakout to new highs as the market's cleanest REIT setup.

milestones.label: Recent weeks
milestones.note: Breakout to fresh highs, TradingView's top rating confirmed

risks: REITs broadly are rate-sensitive
~39x forward earnings is a full multiple for a REIT
catalysts: Further accretive acquisitions in target markets
watch: Industrial vacancy trends in coastal gateway markets
critical.valuation: Premium multiple reflecting best-in-class portfolio quality and location scarcity.

analyses.name: Fortinet, Inc.
analyses.verdict: A held position confirming new highs — hold/add signal, not a fresh entry catalyst.

financials.summary: Fortinet's cybersecurity platform (network security, SASE, and unified SecOps) continued to grow at a healthy clip, though growth has decelerated from its historical pace as the firewall-refresh super-cycle matures; billings and margins remained healthy.
highlights: Revenue growth of roughly 20% YoY
Continued SASE/unified-SecOps platform momentum
Healthy operating margins maintained

accomplishments: Continued expansion of the SASE and secure-networking platform beyond legacy firewall hardware
Stock confirming RS new highs with a clean VCP

journey_52w.summary: FTNT worked through a growth-deceleration digestion period earlier in the cycle as the firewall-refresh wave normalized, then re-accelerated on SASE/platform momentum to confirm fresh highs.

milestones.label: Earlier in cycle
milestones.note: Growth deceleration from the firewall hardware-refresh super-cycle

milestones.label: Recent months
milestones.note: SASE/platform momentum drove a fresh breakout to new highs

risks: RSI 65
Growth decelerating to ~20% vs. its historical faster pace
catalysts: Firewall-refresh cycle commentary at next earnings
watch: Next earnings call commentary on the refresh cycle and SASE bookings
critical.valuation: ~46x forward earnings — a premium multiple that requires the platform story to keep delivering.

analyses.name: Space Exploration Technologies Corp. (SpaceX)
analyses.verdict: New listing — lead with the business/valuation thesis, not the quant signals, which are blank given only ~14 trading days of history.

financials.period: Pre/at-IPO (public financial history not yet available)
financials.summary: SpaceX is an extremely new listing (roughly two weeks of trading history) with a market cap north of $2 trillion, reflecting its dominant position in commercial launch (Falcon 9/Heavy reuse economics), Starship development, and the rapidly scaling Starlink satellite-internet business, which is increasingly the larger revenue driver relative to core launch services. As a brand-new public company, standard trailing-fiscal-year GAAP disclosures are not yet meaningfully comparable, and consensus forward estimates imply a very rich multiple (fwd P/E in the 800x+ range) typical of a high-growth, pre-full-profitability platform business at IPO.
highlights: Starlink subscriber and revenue growth is the primary near-term financial driver, increasingly larger than launch-services revenue
Falcon 9 reuse economics continue to dominate global commercial launch market share
Extremely rich forward multiple reflects a long-duration growth story, not near-term earnings

accomplishments: Successful IPO/public listing as SPCX, one of the largest and most anticipated in market history
Continued Falcon 9 launch cadence and reuse-economics leadership
Ongoing Starship test-flight program progress toward a fully reusable next-generation vehicle
Starlink's continued global subscriber and constellation expansion

journey_52w.summary: SPCX only began trading roughly two weeks ago, so there is no 52-week price history yet — the stock has already pulled back about 23% from its post-IPO high of ~$211 to ~$162, reflecting typical post-IPO volatility and price discovery rather than a fundamental deterioration.

milestones.label: IPO
milestones.note: SpaceX began public trading as SPCX at a valuation reflecting its dominance in launch and Starlink

milestones.label: Since listing
milestones.note: Stock has pulled back from its initial post-IPO high as early price discovery plays out

risks: No meaningful technical history yet — VCP/RS/momentum signals are all blank and won't be usable for ~2-3 more months
Extremely rich valuation leaves little room for execution missteps
Starship program schedule/execution risk
Post-IPO lock-up expirations can pressure the stock as more shares become tradeable
watch: Post-IPO price stabilization and volume trends as the stock finds a real trading range
critical.valuation: Priced as a long-duration growth story (Starlink + next-gen launch), not on near-term earnings; treat position sizing accordingly given the volatility of a two-week-old listing.
